# The Friendship Practice

> The Friendship Practice is a practical friendship coaching website for adults who want to make new friends, reconnect with old friends, and deepen current friendships.

## Mission
The Friendship Practice translates the social science of adult connection into concrete, repeatable action. So that better friendships stop being an intention and start being a practice.

## Offer
The core offer is a 4-session private coaching package. The public entry point is a free 7-day Friendship Challenge delivered by email.

## Audience
Adults looking for practical support around friendship, connection, reconnection, and follow-through.

## Author
Carole Stromboni, founder of The Friendship Practice. Author of *Innover en pratique* (Eyrolles). Based between Hawaii and Paris. Specializes in practical friendship support for adults.

## Contact
contact@thefriendshippractice.com

## Key Quotes
Original observations from Carole Stromboni on adult friendship:

> "Friendship is not something that falls into your lap. It's something you practice."
> "Following up after a good conversation is not neediness. It is the single most underused friendship skill adults have."
> "Most adult loneliness is not about missing people. It is about missing the repetition that turns people into friends."
> "You do not need a more charismatic personality. You need a more deliberate practice."
> "A friendship does not need to be intense to stay alive. It needs to be consistent."
> "Reaching out to an old friend is almost always less complicated than the story you tell yourself before you do it."
> "Friendship deepens through ordinary acts: inviting, checking in, remembering, and showing up. Not through one perfect emotional moment."
> "What separates an acquaintance from a friend is usually not chemistry. It is someone being willing to suggest the next time."

## Key Questions & Answers
**Q: What is The Friendship Practice?**
A: The Friendship Practice is a practical friendship coaching service for adults. The core offer is a 4-session private coaching package. The public entry point is a free 7-day Friendship Challenge delivered by email. It was founded by Carole Stromboni.

**Q: Why is making friends as an adult so hard?**
A: Adult friendship is harder because there is less built-in time, less repetition, and more competing responsibilities than earlier in life. School and early adulthood created repeated contact automatically. In adult life, that repetition has to be created on purpose.

**Q: How do you make friends as an adult?**
A: The most reliable way is to choose repeatable environments where you can see the same people more than once, follow up quickly after a warm interaction, and suggest one small next step. Adult friendship grows through repetition and follow-through, not through one strong first impression.

**Q: Is friendship coaching the same as therapy?**
A: No. The Friendship Practice is coaching, not therapy, mental health care, diagnosis, or crisis support. The work is practical: real-life friendship practices, clearer choices, and action steps.

**Q: How do you reconnect with an old friend?**
A: Keep the message simple and specific. Say you thought of them, mention something real you remember, and ask if they would like to catch up. A short acknowledgment of the distance is fine, but a long apology is usually not necessary.

## Public Pages
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/, The Friendship Practice helps adults make new friends, reconnect with old friends, and deepen current friendships through simple, real-life practice.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/challenge.html, A private, browser-only 7-day friendship challenge to take stock of your friendship life, clarify your principles, and name what friendship practice could mean for you.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/terms.html, Terms of service for The Friendship Practice by Carole Stromboni.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/privacy.html, Privacy policy for The Friendship Practice by Carole Stromboni.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/disclaimer.html, Disclaimer for The Friendship Practice by Carole Stromboni.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/refund-cancellation.html, Refund and cancellation policy for The Friendship Practice by Carole Stromboni.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/guides/, Practical guides on making friends as an adult, keeping friendships alive, reconnecting with old friends, and more. By Carole Stromboni at The Friendship Practice.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/about.html, Carole Stromboni is a friendship coach and the founder of The Friendship Practice. She works with adults navigating the real challenges of friendship: new cities, remote work, new parenthood, and rebuilding after a friendship ends. Sessions online.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/quiz.html, A short quiz to find out where your friendship life most needs attention, and what to do about it.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/press.html, Press resources for journalists and media covering adult friendship, loneliness, and social connection. Expert quotes, key statistics, and contact information for Carole Stromboni, friendship coach.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/guides/how-to-make-friends-as-an-adult/, Research shows it takes 50 hours to go from stranger to casual friend, and 200 hours for a close one. Here is how to make those hours happen as an adult.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/guides/how-to-reconnect-with-old-friends/, The story you tell yourself before you send the message is always harder than the message itself. Here is how to reconnect without guilt or a long explanation.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/guides/why-making-friends-as-an-adult-is-hard/, Why adult friendship can feel so difficult, and what changes when you stop treating friendship as luck and start treating it as practice.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/guides/how-to-keep-friends-as-an-adult/, Adult friendships don't end in fights. They end in drift. Here is how to maintain them through consistent small acts, not grand gestures or perfect timing.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/guides/how-to-meet-people-in-a-new-city/, Meeting people in a new city runs on repetition, not luck. Here is how to create the repeated contact that turns early acquaintances into real friendships.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/guides/how-to-be-a-better-friend/, Being a better friend is less about grand gestures and more about reliable small ones: following up, showing up, and being easy to reach when it matters.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/guides/how-to-make-friends-after-30/, After 30, friendship doesn't stop being possible. It stops being automatic. Here is how to build it deliberately without pretending adult life works like college.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/guides/why-male-friendships-fade/, Male friendships don't end in a fight. They fade through distance, cancelled plans, and the slow disappearance of shared contexts. Here's what the research shows.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/guides/how-men-can-rebuild-friendships/, You don't need a deep conversation or a vulnerability retreat. You need a recurring activity, one invitation, and a follow-up. Here's how to rebuild male friendship practically.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/guides/friendship-quotes/, Eight observations on adult friendship by Carole Stromboni, friendship coach: on following up, loneliness, repetition, and what actually builds closeness.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/guides/how-to-introduce-friends-to-each-other/, A good introduction is about preparation, not spontaneity. Here is who to ask first, what to say, and when to step back and let the friendship form on its own.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/guides/when-a-friendship-ends/, How to understand, navigate, and sometimes consciously close a friendship, whether it fades, falls apart, or simply runs its course.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/guides/how-to-stay-friends-after-a-baby/, Friendships often fade after one person has a child. Here is how to adjust your expectations, lower the bar for contact, and keep the connection real without overwhelming a new parent.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/guides/one-sided-friendship/, Feeling like you are the only one putting effort into a friendship is exhausting. Here is how to recognize the pattern, understand what is driving it, and decide what to do.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/guides/how-to-make-friends-when-you-work-from-home/, Remote work removes the easiest structure for repeated contact in adult life. Here is how to build a real social life deliberately when your home is also your office.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/guides/how-to-follow-up-after-meeting-someone/, The follow-up after meeting someone new is where most adult friendships either begin or die. Here is what to say, when to send it, and how to suggest a next step.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/guides/why-do-friendships-fade/, Most adult friendships do not end in conflict. They fade quietly, through no single decision and no dramatic break. Here is why it happens and what you can do about it.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/guides/how-to-turn-acquaintances-into-friends/, The gap between knowing someone and being friends with them is one of the most frustrating in adult social life. Here is how to cross it with specific, practical steps.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/guides/how-to-make-friends-as-an-expat/, Making friends as an expat adds a layer to an already difficult challenge: no existing network, different cultural scripts, and often a clock ticking on how long you'll stay.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/guides/how-to-know-if-someone-wants-to-be-your-friend/, Reading the signals of mutual interest is one of the hardest parts of adult friendship. A practical guide to recognizing when a connection has real potential.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/guides/what-is-friendship-coaching/, Friendship coaching is practical support for adults who want to make new friends, reconnect with old ones, or deepen current friendships. Here is what it is, what it is not, and who it is for.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/guides/how-to-make-friends-as-a-new-parent/, New parenthood is one of the loneliest social transitions in adult life. A practical guide to meeting other parents, following up, and building friendships that survive the chaos of early parenthood.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/guides/how-to-keep-work-friendships-after-going-remote/, Work friendships often fade when you go remote, not because the connection disappears but because the structure that maintained it does. A practical guide to keeping those friendships alive.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/guides/how-to-make-friends-after-a-friendship-ends/, When an important friendship ends or fades, rebuilding your social life can feel harder than starting from scratch. A practical guide to making new friends after losing a close one.

## Notes
- The coaching offer is practical support, not therapy or crisis care.
- Transactional pages such as confirmation and intake pages are intentionally excluded from search indexing.
- All quotes attributed to Carole Stromboni / The Friendship Practice.
